[[File:Saradomin sword detail.png|left|140px]]
The '''Saradomin sword''' is the personal weapon of [[Commander Zilyana]], the [[Saradomin]]ist general within the [[God Wars Dungeon]]. It can be obtained as a rare drop from Commander Zilyana, or as a very rare drop from her bodyguards. It is a [[two-handed sword]] which requires 70 [[Attack]] to wield.

Unlike most two-handed weapons in the game, it has an attack speed of 4, matching the speed of many commonly used melee weapons, such as scimitars and the [[abyssal whip]].

Wielding this sword in the [[Bandit Camp (Kharidian Desert)]] will cause the [[Bandit (Bandit Camp)|bandits]] there to become hostile.

Players can attach [[Saradomin's tear]] to the Saradomin sword to create [[Saradomin's blessed sword]], which has a +18 higher [[slash]], +6 higher [[strength bonus]], a different [[special attack]], and becomes untradeable. This process, however, will cause the sword to be consumed, and cannot be undone.

==Special attack==
[[File:Saradomin's Lightning.gif|frame|A player performing the Saradomin sword's special attack, ''Saradomin's Lightning''.]]
The Saradomin sword has a [[special attacks|special attack]], ''Saradomin's Lightning'', that deals 10% more melee damage and 1-16 extra [[Magic]] damage. This special attack consumes 100% of the wielder's special attack energy.

The special attack rolls against the opponent's [[Slash]] defence bonus using the attack bonus of the stance the player is currently in.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1251844995499335682|date=19 April 2020|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/107475|archivedate=24 December 2022|quote=[Does sara sword always use your slash bonus or the bonus of the mode you're using? (...)] 1. The one you're using, though it rolls against the target's slash defence. That's fairly common with specs. (...)}} If the melee attack misses, then the Magic attack will also fail. Players receive 2 Magic experience for each point of damage caused by the extra Magic damage.

The magical part of ''Saradomin's Lightning'' will always splash when directed at players using [[Protect from Magic]].{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1251844995499335682|date=19 April 2020|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/107475|archivedate=24 December 2022|quote=[(...) And why does the special attack magic hit always splash in pvp when the opponent is protecting from magic?] (...) 2. The dev who made it included a specific check to do that if the target was praying against magic. He's no longer around to ask why.}} The Magic damage will always hit 0 on [[Cyclops|cyclopes]] in the [[Warriors' Guild]], as with all non-melee damage there.

==Use==
The Saradomin sword is identical to the [[abyssal whip]] in strength bonus, slash attack bonus and attack speed whilst also offering a secondary [[crush]] attack style along with a small [[prayer bonus]].

Notably, the sword can be used to train [[Strength]], as unlike the whip, it has an "aggressive" attack style. However, since the whip is a one-handed weapon and can therefore be used in conjunction with a [[shield]] or a [[defender]], the whip is generally the better option unless one wishes to gain Strength experience, or has need for crush attacks.

As several weapons of similar tier were later introduced into ''[[Old School RuneScape]]'', the Saradomin sword has since fallen out of popularity as the "best" weapon for training Strength. However, the sword is still comparable in power to other level 70 weapons, especially when cost efficiency is taken into account.

This weapon is a common selection among [[combat pures]], as their lower [[Defence]] levels leave little options for strength bonus in off-hand items. Furthermore, the Saradomin sword lacks a "controlled" attack style and features an alternate "defensive" attacking animation, both of which can help players avoid inadvertently gaining Defence experience.

==Trivia==
*The Saradomin sword uses the same idle and combat animations as the [[Godsword]].
*When wielding the Saradomin sword, capes worn by the player start waving as if there is a 'heroic' wind present.
